key_files.relevance: _init_backend sends MessageType.ERROR for fatal init failures; needs to use _emit_state_changed
_emit_state_changed: thread-safe state emitter with validation; accepts state, message, fatal params
handle_init already emits loading state at line 124; FR2 is already implemented
handleMessage switch: missing case 'error' branch; falls through to console.warn
handleStateChanged: already handles state='error' + fatal path via handleFatalError
test_init_backend_failure_sends_fatal_error: asserts MessageType.ERROR - will need updating after FR1

patterns.name: state-machine-error-emission
patterns.description: _emit_state_changed(server, 'error', message=str(e), fatal=True) is the correct pattern for fatal errors

patterns.name: non-fatal-operational-errors
patterns.description: Non-fatal errors in handle_accept, handle_update_config, handle_pause_resume use MessageType.ERROR with fatal=False; these are valid

patterns.name: frontend-case-handler
patterns.description: Switch case handlers in handleMessage pass typed payloads to named functions

similar_features.description: emit-loading-state epic already implemented FR2 (loading state emission in handle_init)

architecture.backend_error_paths: Two paths: (1) _emit_state_changed for state-machine transitions, (2) MessageType.ERROR for non-fatal ops
architecture.frontend_error_handling: handleStateChanged handles state='error'; handleFatalError handles fatal=True
architecture.thread_safety: _emit_state_changed acquires _state_lock; _init_backend runs in daemon thread - no deadlock risk since _state_lock is not held when _init_backend is called

extension_points: handleMessage switch in main.ts - add case 'error': before default

testing.frontend_tests: tests/test_html_structure.py (static analysis), no JS unit tests

conventions: Tests must NOT be tailored to production code behavior - production code changes require test updates
Worker errors in _start_image_delivery at line 737 also use MessageType.ERROR with fatal=True - this is also a state-changing error and may need FR1 treatment per FR3 audit
